channel.title: Articles on AfonsoRibeiro
channel.description: Recent content in Articles on AfonsoRibeiro

item.title: Rid your self of internet addiction
item.description:
No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it. - 1 Corinthians 10:13

Feeling like the internet as been taking a lot of your valuable time in recent years? I will share with you some of the ways I found that helped me escape the addiction and hopefully help you too!

why do we spend so much time on the internet?

With the advent of social media and other platforms whose main objective is making money, the internet quickly turned into a place where you spend most your time on said platforms. The more time you spend on these platforms, the more money they make. Therefore, their aim is to make you spend the majority of your time there and, in a way, hack you brain. If you are a seasoned internet addict, will most likely developed an habit to look at your phone when you get bored even if it’s just for an instant or spend countless hours on YouTube or other websites. That’s their doing (and also yours if you continue to let that happen).

The easy way out!

Thankfully, there is a easy way out! Just turn of the internet. No, I’m serious, please do it. This is the cold turkey method. The best way to break an addiction is by not practicing it. It might feel weird the first few days but trust me, it gets easier as you go on. You will find that you have a lot of things you could do instead of spending time on the internet. If you don’t have that feeling than I’d recommend you try to go outside for a walk or read a book you’d like, something to fill your time up.

A quick warning: Don’t fear missing out on something. Most likely anything won’t happen and if anything does happen it most likely it isn’t important. If you are expecting some important message or email than make sure you receive it and turn off the internet immediately. You may spend more time than expected while you are online looking at cute cats or whatever happens to be on your social media feed.

Reducing our addiction while still using the internet

Nowadays, we are dependent on the internet to our work. That gets in our way to reduce our internet usage. We may find ourselves looking at social media and other websites instead of doing the work that we need to get done. And, when things really need to get done, they end up with a lot of problems and we end up with a lot of stress trying to accomplish something in half of the time we had to do it. That’s not healthy to anyone.

Just like above, the answer most people would give you is simple: “Just don’t do it, lol”. But, sometimes, we rely on social media for something. Let’s say we need to watch a tutorial on YouTube. you watch the tutorial you needed but then look at the side bar and see a lot of videos that caught your attention. You feel tempted and, depending how ingrained your habit of watching YouTube is, you may ending up clicking on it and spending the next thirty minutes watching a YouTube video. either way it’s not going to be the only time you are going to be tempted on the platform.

Breaking the chain

What we want to do is to stop somewhere on the chain of actions that lead you to spend countless hours online without any good reason. Let’s take the example I showed above. What could we do that would help us not spend thirty minutes watching a random YouTube video? As mentioned, above you could just use your will power but, for someone who has an ingrained habit, it may take a great amount of will power. Most of the time you, your primitive brain just takes over and you end up watching the video. Instead, We could just cut the videos on the sidebar of YouTube. That surely would help you not spend some more time than desired. There are a few browser addons that can do this but I would recommend unhook. It has been the best one from my personal experience. Just click the link, choose your browser and then install it. Tweak the settings to your desired preferences and you are ready to go!

Creating scarcity

Another way to create is to artificially create scarcity. What do I mean by this? When something is scarce and valuable to us, we tend to be more careful with how we use it, for example, money. If we limit how much time we have access to internet or certain websites we will tend to be careful with how much time is spent there. For this, I would recommend leechblock. Another addon but this one is meant to limit how much time is spent on certain websites. Experiment with it to find a good amount of time where you can comfortably do the things you want but not spend much more time than desired looking at mostly useless things.

Resources

Now that you got a good look how your internet addiction works and got the basis of how to break it, here are some resources to help you with your journey:

StevenBlack’s hosts

it’s a collection of links that contain harmful content (adware, malware, fake news, gambling, porn and social media) organized in a format meant to be blocked. If you store all these links on your computer’s host file, they will be blocked when you browse the internet. They have a tutorial on where to find the hosts file on your system. click here to check it out. There is also this video tutorial if you are interested.

AfonsoRib’s Hosts

Follows the same scheme as above. It’s just a collection of links that I felt were missing from stevenBlack’s hosts. It blocks a bunch of fediverse websites and other frontends for things like reddit and YouTube. You may want to unblock some fediverse instances if you use it actively.

Unhook - Remove YouTube Recommended Videos

As it was said above, This is a browser addon meant to block youtube recommend sections and much more other things meant to sway your attention while on the platform.

LeechBlock

Another addon that was mentioned above. This one is meant to help you not spend more time than desired in a specific websites of your choice.

Contributing

If you this has helped you or you went through the process of reducing internet usage and found some tricks not mentioned here, feel free to contribute! Contribute addons or other resources that make it easier to spend less time online.

If you are reading this on my blog, you can contribute by checking the github repo here.

Whats next?

So you may be wondering: “I’ve done it. I have reduced my internet usage to a point where I’m a healthier person. What’s next?”. What’s written here can also be applied to other parts of your life. It isn’t strictly applied to the internet. I just made it for the internet as it is what I feel like people and myself mostly struggled with nowadays. So try to apply it to other thing in your life and see if you get good results. Good luck!

item.title: Hyperacionalization
item.description:
What is hyperacionality?

One of the characteristics that the enlightenment brought with it was the focus on rationality as a means of reaching truth. From this, a lot of wild and interesting ideas (at first sight) came to be born. Unlike the Greeks who use deductive reasoning, they approached the creation of knowledge using inductive reasoning, that is, through looking for patterns in nature and from that stipulate some rule that explains said phenomena. This isn’t necessarily bad per say; the problem lies with human nature. we tend to be more inclined to belive what it’s close to what we already believe so we, sometimes unknowably, try to prove our beliefs through “reasoning”. That’s how we can reach false conclusions while being “rational”. the logic may be correct, assuming the premises are true but in reality the premises we take for granted aren’t always true. I like to call this hyperacionality; When you use reason so much that you go beyond it. You are, in essence, building a house of cards that’s ready to fall. Let’s see an example of what I’m saying so that we can visualize it better!

The things we take for granted

Some sociological and economical works take for granted that man and woman are fully rational beings. That is, if confronted if two choices they will choose whats best for them. A good example of this would be Adam Smith’s concept of the Invisible Hand. If you have to do groceries what’s the most likely place where you will do it? The Invisible Hand would point you to the best place according to the cost/effectiveness of the product you want. The reality is that it doesn’t always work that way (see my post on markets). People may go to a super market or a small grocery shop instead of going to a regular market where the food is better and cheaper. And why is that? well, in this case I would say that it is out of habit maybe some laziness. Not really a logical reason. If we assume that the person is fully rational, it correctly follows that we would choose our best option. While observing the real world and not the world of our ideas we conclude that humans aren’t all that rational. The body is just a machine of vices if you don’t take a hold of it.

Using reason to prove our beliefs

Let’s say we have something that we believe in and, because of the ideas instilled on us by the enlightenment, don’t want it to be just an act of faith. Let’s take the example of communism. Marx didn’t find out about how and why communism was going to happen simply by studying economics and noticing some never noticed patterns in the graphs. He already had some set of beliefs and wanted to formalize it in paper so that others could see what he sees. This is all and good but he, like the previous example, takes for granted that humans are strictly rational beings. So, following the ideas mention in his books, which also were rational, they would end up in a communist utopia and that there was no other way. Reality is… It didn’t happen. “But dude, what happened in the soviet union was {insert excuse here}”. this is the most common criticism I see and it’s just shifting blame to something else which is funny because it also is racionalization that proves a persons belief. With this, I don’t intend to say that all that is arrived at through reason, even if the person already believed in that before, is false. There may be good material written in Marx’s books but some of his conclusions are plagued by hyperracionalism.

This has also happened to me. I’ve tried to justify my wrong doings with reason. Something on the line of “I can take a bit more of this because what they are paying is a steal!”. After noticing it I just decided to stop buying there or buy there but take what I am allowed.

Reason as a tool

With all this talk looks like I am making a bad picture out of reason. this is not the case. I just want more people to recognize that reason is not what it’s made out to be. Reason is a tool. A great one. But, just like a hammer. It can hurt pretty badly if we are not careful.

item.title: Go to Your Local Market
item.description:
For the last two months I’ve switching from going mostly to my nearest supermarket to the local market. This has been one of my best recent decisions and I expect to carry this through the rest of my life. Not only has it cut the time I had to walk to the nearest supermarket, it also made me save a good amount of money while getting much better products.

I am currently living in an apartment near my university and, as of this moment, the nearest supermarket is a 10 to 15 minute walk from where I live. Near my apartment there is this market which I have mostly neglected for over a year and a half. Not necessarily because it was bad but, during the covid hysteria, it was only open during the Saturday while the supermarkets were open all week for some stupid reason. This was not really useful for me as I sometimes would wake up late and miss the opportunity to buy there. So, out of habit, I just went to the supermarket.

The covid hysteria started dying down and soon the market was open all week. I decided to give it a try since I despise these megacorps who poison our food and soon found out that, not only was the food way cheaper (sometimes 3 times cheaper than the supermarket counterpart), came mostly from local sources and the products were just better overall! Of course, somethings that I ate were usually not available there but the things that I needed to make most of my meals were. I still rely on the supermarket for those things (mainly milk, yogurt, oats) but I have to take way less trips there. Now, I only which that I could get these things from the market.

The last thing that surprised me was the meat. Although it is just a tad more costly, for the quality it’s a really great deal. Unexpectedly, a few things, like hamburgers or minced meat, are way cheaper for some reason. the meat is a lot different and way tastier that the antibiotic and corn fed meat from the supermarket. With the money I save on vegetables and fruit, I feel like buying meat form the butcher is good idea. I like to think of it as a kind of insure so that I reduce the risk of getting some weird disease or cancer from the poisoned supermarket meat.

item.title: Technique is going to enslave us all! - A brief view of Jacques Ellul's The Technological Society
item.description:
Watch it in a video format here.

Introduction

Technique is going to enslave us all! We, in the modern world, like to think that we are in control of ourselves. We tell ourselves that we are using Technique, most of the time misunderstood as technology, for our own good. That it is all in good will and for the betterment of mankind. When, In reality, It’s pretty much opposite. We are slaves to it.

What is Technique?

What then is “Technique”? Technique, in ellul’s words, is “the totality of methods rationally arrived at and having absolute efficiency (for a given stage of development) in every field of human activity”. There is, for example, the economical technique, the propaganda technique and political technique, All working together and dependent on one another.

What It Does To Us?

More and more we seek maximum efficiency in all fields of human activity and we tend to think that is because we want to, to make our lives better. We try to rationalize economies and apply theories so that it can potentially yield better results. We vote for politicians who we believe are going to make our life conditions better. We spend enormous amounts of time binge watching the latest shows so we can forget about how boring and meaningless our work and life really is.

This search for maximum efficiency makes us slaves to technique, contrary to the popular notion that we are using it. Technique develops by itself and for it itself. We are forced to accept technical advancement or else we are put at a disadvantage compared to those who have accepted it. There is only an illusion of choice in our technical society. For example, the President doesn’t really have much power as he is limited by technique. May he dare to oppose it and he will suffer the consequences. How, then, does the president explain that the plan that he envisioned and set out to do, ignoring what technique said, failed to come about? No one is going to be happy with it and soon the President will be no more, replaced by someone else more inline with what technique expects of him.

In the economical sphere, almost everybody is convinced of the effectiveness of planning (that is, to an extent). Even some Hayek fanboys tend to be tempted the allow at least some minimal intervention on the markets. We have increasingly more complex and, over time, possibly more accurate models of our economy as technique is perfected. As of now, economical technique, as with other techniques, it is still in its infancy and the results of it’s application still have a lot of uncertainty.

As technique advances, the tendency is to create a more centralized and totalitarian government with a totally planned economy. This is due to the fact that, as technique progresses, it tends to become more and more expensive. As it becomes more expensive, the only institutions with enough wealth to afford expanding technique tend to be monopolistic corporations with a vast amount of wealth and the state. Because of this, Technique prefers to centralize wealth so that it can maximize the funds for its development. A good example of this type of system is China, where the state has ownership of the companies and can choose to invest in a particular area with the help of said companies, for example, Huawei.

Of course, this brings with it unnatural and negative consequences for the individual. For that reason it is necessary to control and satisfy his basic needs so that he doesn’t revolt against the system. In the most severe cases, mass surveillance and state force is used to take control of the situation but, as of now, this method is expensive and not a good solutions long term. Propaganda and education techniques are employed to steer people in the direction that technique wants them to take before any extreme measure is taken. This is to prepare the individual for a great amount of meaningless work and stay still for 8 hours in a desk. Although he is only an object in technique’s view, in a similar idea to Marx’s labour-power, as just a mere commodity to be traded, easily illustrated by the new human resources department of hip and cool companies, he is still human and may not initially be integrated totally to the technological system. May he fail to integrate and become neurotic, the technical system will employ the use of mind altering techniques such as psychology to reintegrate the individual back into the system. Even if what is causing the distress in the person is a byproduct of the technical system, a psychologist won’t point that out, as his is job aiding the individual to be reintegrated back into the technical system. In specific cases, The technical system may resort to the use drugs, doping the individual to be capable of handling the extreme negative effects of the situation he is in and reduce the possibility of breaking down, somewhat similar to lube in a machine. A good example of this is the use of stimulants by the Nazis in the second world war. The soldier were doped with drugs, to endure the fatigue caused by the intensity of blitz krieg.

The technical system makes many, especially young people, to feel aimless, without any purpose in their life. They don’t know what they want to do in the future. This is due to the technique’s increased demand for specialized work, as technique becomes more and more complex. This forces people to specialize. something people didn’t do has much as the times we live in. Vocational guidance was the technical solution developed for this problem. The big difference between the mainstream idea of vocational guidance and the technical counterpart is that the mainstream idea is based on finding the aptitude of the individual and the technical is focused on the adaptability of the individual to what the technical system desires. This has lead to the fields of Humanities and Arts to be considered less valuable than more technical fields. This is reflected when we say jokingly that arts is a waste of time cause and it’s going to leave us homeless. The emphasis of vocational guidance is on insuring the happiness of the individual through adaptation. Vocational guidance is yet another technique to integrate the individual without force into the technical system.

By way of propaganda, technique shapes and molds us without our consent or even awareness that it is taking place. It suppresses our critical faculties, not necessarily because we refuse to acknowledge it but simply because it was put into our heads and everyone around us seems to think in a similar way. this is due to technique acting upon the collective. In turn, suppressing the individuals critical faculties leads to a good social conscience. The individual finds himself agreeing with his friends and workers, thinking that they are all partaking in something worthwhile. This is greatly reflected on Nazi Germany and the communist regime and even the United States during the second world war. In case the propaganda technique fails, it leads to a great increase in neurotic behavior. These reasons give technique a sacred character. We aren’t allowed to question it. We cease to know what is true and what is false and we’d rather go with the flow than being left behind as we are addicted to it as if it was a drug.

Thus, Technique artificially induces a new zone of the forbidden, in contrast to taboos of primitive society which emerge naturally. A good representation of this is the overton window, where, the window’s position on the political spectrum dictates what is deemed acceptable to talk about and discuss. Outside of the window is what is considered radical, taboo or simply wrong, not worth discussing. Although simplistic, the overton window reflects the core concept of ellul’s idea of the sacred.

The final goal of technique is to totally integrate and subjugate the individual. It touches all fields of human activity. His work life, social life, his psychology… everything. This may lead to the total separation of men from real world. This quote from the book best illustrates this: “One of our best-known specialists in diseases of the nervous system writes: “We will be able to modify man’s emotions, desires and thoughts, as we have already done in a rudimentary way with tranquilizers.” It will be possible, says our specialist to produce a conviction or an impression of happiness without any real basis for it. Our man of the golden age, therefore, will be capable of “happiness” amid the worst privations. Why, then, promise us extraordinary comforts, hygiene, knowledge, and nourishment if, by simply manipulating our nervous systems, we can be happy without them? The last meager motive we could possibly ascribe to the technical adventure thus vanishes into thin air through the very existence of technique itself.”

Final Notes

The book offers no hope for what is ahead of us, may we follow down this path. I personally believe that there is a possibility which is something I plan on expanding on. But the mere existence of this book, The technological society, is a hint that there is still hope.
